ksa_text: Ability to identify, assess, and prioritize risks in AI systems and governance contexts; develop mitigation strategies calibrated to organizational risk tolerance and operational constraints.

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Capability building for digital, data and technology

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Although AI Governance is an edge domain for the Director of Data Products, this ability KSA presents sufficient signal (composite score 0.507) and conceptual alignment to include at the director level.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (AI Governance), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.507). External reviewers may question whether this specific ability KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the Director of Data Products versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act (Article 9), and OECD AI Principles confirm AI governance as a critical competency for responsible AI leadership.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with AI Governance appearing in job postings and role definition literature for Director of Data Products.

decision_rationale: Edge-domain AI Governance ability KSA cleared adversarial review with score 0.507. Included based on sufficient framework alignment and role relevance for the Director of Data Products.
